formulas.name: Electrophilic Aromatic Substitution (EAS)
formulas.variables: Ar - Aromatic ring, X - Halogen, HX - Hydrogen halide
formulas.notes: The EAS reaction involves the substitution of a hydrogen atom in an aromatic ring by a halogen atom.

formulas.name: Halogenation of Alkanes
formulas.variables: R - Alkyl group, X - Halogen, HX - Hydrogen halide
formulas.notes: Halogenation of alkanes is the reaction of an alkane with a halogen to form a haloalkane.

formulas.name: Nucleophilic Substitution Reaction
formulas.formula: R-X + Nu: → R-Nu + X: (Primary) or R-X + Nu: → R-Nu + X2 (Secondary)
formulas.variables: R - Alkyl group, X - Halogen, Nu - Nucleophile
formulas.notes: The nucleophilic substitution reaction involves the replacement of a halogen atom in an alkyl halide by a nucleophile.

formulas.name: SN1 Reaction Mechanism
formulas.variables: R - Alkyl group, X - Halogen, Nu - Nucleophile
formulas.notes: The SN1 reaction mechanism involves a two-step process: the formation of a carbocation and its subsequent reaction with a nucleophile.

formulas.name: SN2 Reaction Mechanism
formulas.formula: R-X + Nu: → R-Nu + X: (Primary) or R-X + Nu: → R-Nu + X2 (Secondary)
formulas.variables: R - Alkyl group, X - Halogen, Nu - Nucleophile
formulas.notes: The SN2 reaction mechanism involves a concerted, single-step process where the nucleophile attacks the alkyl halide from the back.

formulas.name: Cis-Trans Isomerism in Haloalkanes
formulas.notes: Cis-trans isomerism occurs in haloalkanes with two or more halogen atoms on adjacent carbon atoms, where the arrangement of the halogen atoms can be either cis (same side) or trans (opposite side).

formulas.name: Stereoisomerism in Haloalkanes
formulas.notes: Stereoisomerism in haloalkanes occurs due to the presence of chiral centers (asymmetric carbon atoms) and can result in enantiomers (mirror images) and diastereomers (non-mirror images).

formulas.name: Electrophilic Aromatic Substitution (EAS) Orientation
formulas.variables: o/p - Ortho/Para, 3,5 and 2,6 - Specific positions on the aromatic ring
formulas.notes: The orientation of the electrophilic aromatic substitution (EAS) reaction in haloarenes depends on the directing effect of the substituents on the aromatic ring.
